Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOSUE LOPEZ'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions.

The motion is DENIED. The plaintiff misreads the requirements of Form Interrogatory No. 15.1. The interrogatory is stated in the alternative - i.e., "state all facts upon which you base the denial or special or affirmative defense. . . " (emphasis added). The defendant's Answer contains affirmative defenses and did not plead any specific denials of allegations. As such, their responses are adequate. The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 to Judge Pro Tem Noah Lebowitz by email at noah@ndllegal.com. Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
